title: Image Analysis Algorithm for Co-Migrated DNA Fragments in PFGE Profiles
author: Ibrahim El-khalil Adam
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bthtnj6n

steps.title: Open PFGE image of interest
steps.instruction: Open PFGE image of interest. You should navigate to containing folder first.

steps.title: It is critical to tell the...
steps.instruction: It is critical to tell the program if DNA bands are white in a black background or otherwise.

steps.title: Define lanes and then bands
steps.instruction: Define lanes and then bands. sometimes, manual modification is necessary.

steps.title: Select the lane that shows the DNA ladder
steps.instruction: Select the lane that shows the DNA ladder. then indicate each band with its corresponding size. ( Sizes are in kilo base-pair (kbp). you should multiply each value by 1000 to get accurate results ).

steps.title: CALULATING EXPECTED PIXEL DENSITIES FOR CO-MIGRATED...
steps.instruction: CALULATING EXPECTED PIXEL DENSITIES FOR CO-MIGRATED DNA FRAGMENTS This step is critical. it is based on end-user's estimation. Keep in mind that co-migrated fragments show high pixel densities (Intensity profiles). band intensities should reduce as you go down the profile (toward smaller band sizes). The red line roughly indicate expected band intensities across the whole profile.

steps.title: Copy all the data from "Analysis info
steps.instruction: Copy all the data from "Analysis info." from GelAnalyzer2010a. Press Ctrl + A to copy data. Notice that "Raw volumes" are intensity profiles corresponding each band size "MW".

steps.title: Open PAST statistics
steps.instruction: Open PAST statistics. The goal is to get a polynomial equation with significantly high R 2 value. Past the data copied from GelAnalyzer2010a and past it into PAST statistics. Change column attributes to indicate (Band size) and (Pixel density).

steps.title: Create a preliminary polynomial fit
steps.instruction: Create a preliminary polynomial fit. R 2 value at this point roughly indicates number of co-migrated fragment across the profile. Notice that R2 value is 0.67113.

steps.title: Refer to step 2 (remove values above the red line)
steps.instruction: Refer to step 2 (remove values above the red line). Recreate the polynomial fit and check R 2 value. Remove band sizes and their corresponding pixel densities from both columns ( According to ODD values of pixel densities) . R2 now is 0.98739. Bands removed; 2 to 5, 10 and15 to 18. The polynomial equation we need is: EPD = -6.651E-11*BS^2+0.0013*BS +191.2 EPD: expected pixel density, BS: band size

steps.title: DENOTATION OF ALL BAND SIZES INTO...
steps.instruction: DENOTATION OF ALL BAND SIZES INTO TE EQUATION (from step 2.4): This step requires the same two columns from step 2.2. Open Microsoft Office™ Excel. Past the two previously mentioned columns. Start denotation of column "Band size" into the equation. Use an empty column. Denotation process. Expected pixel densities are ready. notice how close expected and observed PDs are close for bands number 1,8, 9 and 11. we can safely conclude that they represent single DNA fragments. While expected PD of band number 2 is half the observed one.

steps.title: In order to calculate approximate number...
steps.instruction: In order to calculate approximate number of DNA fragments represented by each PFGE band, we must simply divide observed PDs by the corresponding ones. column is named "O/E" indicating observed/expected.

steps.title: FCMs are simply truncated values of O/E column
steps.instruction: FCMs are simply truncated values of O/E column. Here is a screen-recorded video showing the entire protocol in action :
